context:
<Profile>### IDENTITY
- **Name:** Linda Martinez  
- **Age:** 38  
- **Gender:** Female  
- **Experience level:** **Novice/Beginner** — new to consistent structured training (**as of 2026-02-27**)

---

### GOALS
- **Primary goal(s):**
  - **Lose weight and improve body composition** (reduce body fat, build/maintain lean muscle)
- **Secondary goals:**
  - **Increase daily energy** to keep up with kids
  - Build a sustainable routine that works with a single-parent schedule
- **Target events/deadlines:** None specified

---

### TRAINING CONTEXT

#### Schedule & Availability
- **Desired training frequency:** **3 sessions/week**
- **Session duration preferences/constraints:** Not specified (constraint noted: schedule is limited/unpredictable)
- **Time windows:** Not specified
- **Blocked days:** Not specified
- **Notes:** Single parent; schedule is **very limited and unpredictable**, so training should support short, flexible sessions and “minimum effective dose” consistency.

#### Equipment & Environment
- **Primary training location:** **Home gym (garage)**
- **Available equipment:** **Kettlebells, resistance bands**
- **Missing/unknown details (for programming accuracy):**
  - Kettlebell weights available (exact lbs/kg)
  - Band types/tensions (mini bands, long loop bands, tube bands)
  - Space constraints (floor space, overhead clearance)

#### Constraints
- **[ACTIVE]** Postpartum core issues — needs core modifications (**since 2026-02-27**). Avoid/modify high-pressure core work (e.g., aggressive crunch/sit-up volume, long planks if symptomatic) and prioritize rehab-style core stability progressions.
- **[ACTIVE]** Very limited and unpredictable schedule (**since 2026-02-27**). Program should be modular (10–30 minute options), low setup, and flexible week-to-week.

#### Preferences
- Training style preferences: Not specified
- Movement likes/dislikes: Not specified
- Communication style preferences: Not specified
- Focus areas (implied by goals/constraints): Full-body strength + conditioning; postpartum-safe core; efficiency and adherence

---

### METRICS
*(No baseline performance or body composition measurements provided yet — metrics below are placeholders to be filled once available.)*

**Body Composition (priority: weight loss / body comp)**
- **Body weight:** Not provided (**as of 2026-02-27**)
- **Waist/hip measurements:** Not provided (**as of 2026-02-27**)
- **Photos or fit-of-clothes marker:** Not provided (**as of 2026-02-27**)

**Strength**
- No current lift or rep baselines provided (**as of 2026-02-27**)

**Endurance**
- No cardio baseline provided (**as of 2026-02-27**)

**Movement Quality**
- Postpartum core status specifics not provided (**as of 2026-02-27**): symptoms (doming/coning, heaviness/pressure, pain), diastasis recti status, tolerance to planks/dead bugs/carries, etc.

---

### LOG
## 2026-02-27 — Profile created/updated (intake)
- Added goals: weight loss + improved body composition; more energy for kids.
- Training context captured: **3x/week** desired frequency; **home garage** setup with **kettlebells + bands**.
- Constraints added: **postpartum core modifications** and **unpredictable schedule**.
- Metrics section left blank pending baseline data (weight/measurements and simple performance baselines).</Profile>

<Plan>## Header
- **Program name:** Garage Minimal-Effective-Dose Body Recomp (Postpartum-Safe) — 3 Days/Week
- **Program Owner:** GymText
- **User:** Linda Martinez
- **Goal:** Lose weight and improve body composition (reduce body fat, build/maintain lean muscle)
- **Duration:** Ongoing with phase cycling

---

## Program Philosophy
Linda is a beginner returning to consistent training with a very limited, unpredictable schedule and postpartum core considerations. This program uses **3 short, full-body sessions** built around **kettlebells + bands** with **low setup and high carryover**: squat/hinge/push/pull/carry plus postpartum-safe core progressions. Each day is written in **modules** so Linda can do a **10-, 20-, or 30-minute** version without feeling like she “failed” the workout. Progress is primarily **reps → load → density (more work in less time)** using **RPE** to autoregulate around sleep/stress, while core work stays **symptom-guided** (no coning/doming, heaviness, pain, or leaking).

---

# Phase 1: Foundation & Core Rebuild (Weeks 1–4)

### Weekly Pattern (3 days/week, flexible)
Do these as **Day A / Day B / Day C** in order. If the week gets chaotic, just do the “next day” when you can.

**Intensity guide (whole phase):** mostly **RPE 6–7** (2–4 reps in reserve).  
**Session options:**  
- **10 min:** Warm-up (1 min) + Main Block only  
- **20 min:** Warm-up + Main Block + Accessory Block  
- **30 min:** Warm-up + Main + Accessory + Core/Finisher

---

## Day A — Full Body (Hinge + Push + Carry)
- **Focus:** posterior chain, simple pressing, posture, carries
- **Volume:** ~10–16 working sets (depending on 20 vs 30 min version)

**Warm-up (3–4 min)**
- 90/90 breathing or crocodile breathing: 4–6 slow breaths — *sets ribcage/pelvis to reduce core pressure*
- Hip hinge drill to wall: 8 reps
- Band pull-aparts (light): 12–15 reps

**Main Block (12–15 min)**
- **Kettlebell Deadlift (from floor or elevated):** 3 × 6–10 @ RPE 6–7  
  *foundational hinge pattern; easy to load safely and builds calorie-burning muscle*
- **Band or KB Floor Press:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 6–7  
  *pressing strength without long planks/push-up pressure if that’s symptomatic*
- **1-Arm Band Row or 1-Arm KB Row (supported on bench/box):** 3 × 10–14 @ RPE 7  
  *upper-back strength for posture and “mom-life” carrying endurance*

**Accessory Block (optional, 6–8 min)**
- **Glute Bridge (bodyweight or band):** 2 × 10–15 @ RPE 6–7  
- **Band Face Pull:** 2 × 12–20 @ RPE 7

**Core (postpartum-safe) (optional, 4–6 min)**
- **Dead Bug (heel taps or arms only):** 2 × 6–8/side (slow)  
  *anti-extension control with low pressure when done correctly*
- **Suitcase Carry (light KB):** 4 × 20–40 steps (alternate sides)  
  *real-life core + grip + posture; usually very well tolerated postpartum*

---

## Day B — Full Body (Squat + Pull + Conditioning)
- **Focus:** knee-dominant strength + pulling + simple conditioning
- **Volume:** ~10–18 working sets

**Warm-up (3–4 min)**
- Box squat sit-to-stand (bodyweight): 8 reps
- Band rows (light): 12 reps
- March in place (tall posture): 30–45 sec

**Main Block (12–15 min)**
- **Goblet Squat to Box (or target):** 3 × 6–10 @ RPE 6–7  
  *box target helps consistency and confidence as a beginner*
- **Band Lat Pulldown (anchored overhead) OR Band Row:** 3 × 10–15 @ RPE 7  
- **Kettlebell Romanian Deadlift:** 2–3 × 8–12 @ RPE 6–7  
  *hamstrings/glutes for body comp and back support*

**Accessory/Conditioning Block (optional, 6–10 min)**
Choose one:
- **Low-impact KB complex (easy):** 6–10 minutes continuous, alternate:
  - 5 KB deadlifts
  - 5 goblet squats
  - 5 band rows  
  Keep it **easy-moderate (RPE 6)**, breathe through reps.
  
OR
- **Intervals (garage-friendly):** 6–10 rounds:
  - 30 sec brisk step-ups (or marching fast)
  - 30–60 sec easy walk/breathe

**Core (optional, 3–5 min)**
- **Side Plank (knees bent):** 2 × 15–25 sec/side @ easy effort  
  *short holds reduce pressure while building lateral core endurance*

---

## Day C — Full Body (Single-Leg + Overhead/Angle Press + Back)
- **Focus:** balance, hips, shoulders, “athletic mom strength”
- **Volume:** ~10–16 working sets

**Warm-up (3–4 min)**
- Hip airplanes (supported) or standing hip circles: 5/side
- Band external rotation (elbows at sides): 10–15 reps
- Light suitcase carry: 20 steps/side

**Main Block (12–15 min)**
- **Split Squat (bodyweight → goblet as tolerated):** 3 × 6–10/side @ RPE 6–7  
  *single-leg strength improves daily-life stamina and reduces “one side does everything” habits*
- **Half-Kneeling Band Press (or KB press if comfortable):** 3 × 8–12/side @ RPE 6–7  
  *half-kneeling position helps core control and reduces compensation*
- **Band Row (different grip than Day A/B):** 3 × 10–15 @ RPE 7

**Accessory Block (optional, 6–8 min)**
- **Banded Good Morning (light/moderate):** 2 × 12–20 @ RPE 7  
- **Biceps Curl + Triceps Pressdown (bands):** 1–2 × 12–20 each (superset)

**Core (optional, 3–6 min)**
- **Bird Dog (hands elevated on a box if needed):** 2 × 6–8/side (3-sec reach)  
  *anti-rotation + spinal control, typically low symptom risk*

---

## Progression Strategy (Phase 1)
**Goal:** learn movements, build consistency, and increase work capacity without core symptom flare-ups.

1. **Double progression (main lifts):**
   - Start at the low end of the rep range.
   - Add **1–2 reps per set** week to week until you hit the top of the range for all sets.
   - Then increase load slightly (heavier KB, stronger band, or more band tension) and return to the low end.

2. **RPE guardrails:**
   - Most sets at **RPE 6–7**.
   - If sleep is poor or stress is high, keep the same load and do fewer reps (still a win).

3. **Deload (Week 4):**
   - Reduce sets by ~40–50% (e.g., 3 sets → 2 sets), keep loads similar, stay at **RPE 6**.
   - Keep core work easy and high-quality.

4. **Core symptoms rule (non-negotiable):**
   - If you notice **doming/coning, heaviness/pressure, pain, or leaking**, reduce range of motion, shorten holds, elevate hands (incline), or swap to breathing + carries.

**Transition to Phase 2 when:**  
- You’ve completed ~3 solid weeks (not necessarily perfect), movements feel more automatic, and you can finish sessions without symptom flare-ups.

---

# Phase 2: Build Strength + Lean Muscle (Weeks 5–8)

### Weekly Pattern
Same **A/B/C** structure for simplicity. Slightly more challenge and a bit more density.

**Intensity guide:** **RPE 7–8** on last set of main lifts (1–3 reps in reserve).  
**Volume:** slightly higher (or same volume, more load).

## Key Upgrades (apply across days)
- **KB Deadlift → KB Sumo Deadlift or Tempo Deadlift (3 sec down):** 3–4 × 6–8 @ RPE 7  
  *tempo increases stimulus even if KBs are not super heavy*
- **Goblet Squat:** 3–4 × 6–10 @ RPE 7–8
- **Row variation:** 3–4 × 8–12 @ RPE 7–8
- Add **one “finish strong” block** 1–2 days/week (optional):
  - **EMOM 8 minutes (easy-moderate):**
    - Minute 1: 8 goblet squats
    - Minute 2: 10 band rows
    - Repeat x4

## Core Progression (still symptom-guided)
Choose 1–2 per session:
- **Dead Bug → Dead Bug with exhale emphasis:** 2–3 × 6–10/side
- **Side plank (knees) → side plank (full) if symptom-free:** 2 × 15–30 sec/side
- **Suitcase carry:** progress distance or load gradually

---

## Progression Strategy (Phase 2)
- **Weeks 5–7:** add either
  - **+1 set** to one main lift per day (if time allows), *or*
  - **+1–2 reps** per set, *or*
  - Slight band tension/load increase
- **Week 8 deload:** reduce sets ~40–50%, keep intensity moderate (RPE 6–7).
- Move to Phase 3 when you feel you’re recovering well and want a stronger “fat-loss engine” (more density/conditioning).

---

# Phase 3: Density & Conditioning (Weeks 9–12)

### Weekly Pattern
Still 3 days/week, but each day includes a **time-capped circuit** to increase calorie burn and work capacity without requiring long sessions.

**Intensity guide:** strength work **RPE 7–8**, circuits **RPE 6–7** (you should finish feeling better, not crushed).

## Day A (Strength + Circuit)
- **KB Deadlift:** 4 × 5–8 @ RPE 7–8  
- **Floor Press:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 7  
- **Row:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 7–8  
- **Circuit 8–12 min (repeat smoothly):**
  - 8 goblet squats (light/mod)
  - 10 band rows
  - 20–40 steps farmer carry (or suitcase carry)

## Day B (Legs + Low-Impact Intervals)
- **Goblet Squat (or split squat):** 4 × 6–10 @ RPE 7–8  
- **RDL:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 7  
- **Intervals 10–15 min:** 30 sec brisk work / 60 sec easy (step-ups, marching, or KB deadlift light)

## Day C (Full-body “ladder”)
- **Ladder 10–15 min (stay submaximal):**
  - 1–2–3–4–5 reps (repeat) of:
    - KB deadlift
    - Half-kneeling band press (each side counts separately; keep reps lower)
    - Band row  
  Stop if core symptoms appear; keep breathing steady.

**Core:** keep 1–2 drills/session, prioritize carries + dead bug variations.

---

## Progression Strategy (Phase 3)
- Progress **density first**:
  - Do the same work in **less time**, or
  - Add **one round** to a circuit while keeping RPE controlled
- Strength lifts progress using double progression as before.
- **Week 12 deload:** reduce circuits by ~50% time and reduce lifting sets by ~40%.

---

## Phase Cycling
**Default cycle:**  
**Foundation (4 wks) → Build (4 wks) → Density (4 wks) → repeat**

**How to repeat (simple rules):**
- If life is chaotic: repeat **Phase 1** or keep Phase 2 but use 20-minute versions.
- If recovery is good and you want faster body comp changes: spend more time in **Phase 3** (e.g., 4–8 weeks), but keep circuit intensity moderate.
- If any postpartum symptoms worsen: revert core work to earlier regressions and keep lifts at **RPE ≤7** until stable.

**Minimum Effective Dose rule (for tough weeks):**
- Aim for **2 sessions/week** minimum:
  - Do **Day A + Day B** one week, **Day B + Day C** the next (rotate).
- If only **1 session**, do **Day A** (most complete balance of hinge/push/pull/carry).

---

## Modification History
- **2026-02-27:** Initial program created to match Linda’s profile: 3x/week home-gym (kettlebells + bands), postpartum core-safe progressions, and modular 10–30 minute session options due to unpredictable single-parent schedule.

--- 

If you tell me (1) what kettlebell weights you have and (2) whether planks/dead bugs/carries cause any symptoms (doming, heaviness, pain), I can lock in starting loads, pick the best press variation, and tailor the core progression more precisely.</Plan>

<Week># Microcycle — Week of February 23, 2026

**Program:** Garage Minimal-Effective-Dose Body Recomp (Postpartum-Safe) — 3 Days/Week  
**Phase:** Phase 1: Foundation & Core Rebuild (Week 1 of 4)  
**User:** Linda Martinez  

## Schedule
- **Mon (2/23):** Day A — Full Body (Hinge + Push + Carry) (home garage)
- **Tue:** Rest / walking (optional)
- **Wed (2/25):** Day B — Full Body (Squat + Pull + Conditioning) (home garage)
- **Thu:** Rest / mobility (optional)
- **Fri (2/27):** Day C — Full Body (Single-Leg + Press Angle + Back) (home garage)
- **Sat:** Rest / easy walk with kids (optional)
- **Sun:** Rest

## Week Overview
Week 1 is about **getting consistent reps with clean form** and keeping effort mostly **RPE 6–7** (you should finish feeling better, not crushed). Because we don’t have your exact kettlebell/band inventory yet, the loads below use **common starter KB jumps** (15/25/35 lb) and **light/medium band**—swap to the closest match you own while keeping the target RPE. Core work is **symptom-guided postpartum-safe**: no doming/coning, heaviness/pressure, pain, or leaking—scale immediately if any show up.

---

# Workout — Monday, February 23, 2026
**Focus:** Day A — Hinge + Push + Carry  
**Location:** Home garage  
**Duration:** ~25–30 minutes (with options for 10/20 minutes)

## Warm-Up (4 minutes)
1. **Crocodile breathing (or 90/90 breathing):** 5 slow breaths (inhale through nose, long exhale)
2. **Hip hinge drill to wall:** 8 reps (pause 1 sec when you “tap” the wall)
3. **Band pull-aparts (light band):** 15 reps (ribcage down; don’t flare)

## Main Workout
### 1. Kettlebell Deadlift (from floor or slightly elevated)
**Target:** 3 × 8 @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 25 lb × 8 (RPE 6)
- **Set 2:** 25 lb × 8 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 3:** 25 lb × 8 (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 90 sec between working sets  
**Notes:** Exhale as the bell leaves the floor; stop the set if you feel core pressure building. If 25 lb is too light to reach RPE 6 by set 3, use **35 lb** next time.

### 2. Kettlebell Floor Press (or Band Floor Press)
**Target:** 3 × 10 @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 15 lb (each hand) × 10 (RPE 6)
- **Set 2:** 15 lb (each hand) × 10 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 3:** 15 lb (each hand) × 10 (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 75–90 sec between working sets  
**Notes:** Keep ribs “knit down.” If using a single KB: do **10/side** with **25 lb**.

### 3. 1-Arm Supported Kettlebell Row (hand on box/bench/chair)
**Target:** 3 × 12/side @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 25 lb × 12/side (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 2:** 25 lb × 12/side (RPE 7)
- **Set 3:** 25 lb × 12/side (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 60–75 sec between sets (after both sides)  
**Notes:** Row to hip pocket; slight pause at top; keep neck long.

## Accessory Block (optional, ~6 minutes)
### 4. Glute Bridge (bodyweight or mini-band above knees)
**Target:** 2 × 12 @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** Bodyweight × 12 (RPE 6)
- **Set 2:** Bodyweight × 12 (RPE 6.5)  
**Rest:** 45–60 sec  
**Notes:** Exhale at the top; avoid back arching.

### 5. Band Face Pull (light-to-medium band)
**Target:** 2 × 15 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** Light band × 15 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 2:** Light band × 15 (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 45–60 sec  
**Notes:** Pull to eyebrow level; elbows high; don’t shrug.

## Core (optional, ~4–5 minutes)
### 6. Dead Bug — Heel Taps (or “arms only” if needed)
**Target:** 2 × 6/side (slow) @ easy-moderate  
- **Set 1:** 6/side (3-sec exhale each rep)
- **Set 2:** 6/side  
**Rest:** 30–45 sec  
**Notes:** If you notice doming/coning, shorten range (tap closer) or switch to **arms-only**.

### 7. Suitcase Carry
**Target:** 4 × 20 steps (alternate sides) @ RPE 6  
- **Set 1:** 25 lb × 20 steps (right)
- **Set 2:** 25 lb × 20 steps (left)
- **Set 3:** 25 lb × 20 steps (right)
- **Set 4:** 25 lb × 20 steps (left)  
**Rest:** 30–45 sec  
**Notes:** Tall posture, slow steps, gentle exhale every 2–3 steps.

## Cool Down (3 minutes)
1. **Child’s pose breathing (wide knees if comfortable):** 5 breaths  
2. **Doorway chest stretch:** 30 sec/side  
3. **Hamstring stretch (gentle, knees soft):** 30 sec/side  

## Notes
- If time is tight: do **Warm-up (1 min breathing + 8 hinges) + Main Lift tri-set only**.
- Total time: ~28 minutes (estimated ~30).

---

# Workout — Wednesday, February 25, 2026
**Focus:** Day B — Squat + Pull + Conditioning  
**Location:** Home garage  
**Duration:** ~25–30 minutes

## Warm-Up (4 minutes)
1. **Box squat sit-to-stand (bodyweight):** 8 reps (controlled down, light touch)
2. **Band rows (light band):** 12 reps
3. **March in place (tall posture):** 40 seconds

## Main Workout
### 1. Goblet Squat to Box/Target
**Target:** 3 × 8 @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 25 lb × 8 (RPE 6)
- **Set 2:** 25 lb × 8 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 3:** 25 lb × 8 (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 90 sec between working sets  
**Notes:** Choose a target that keeps you confident and consistent. Exhale as you stand; avoid bearing down.

### 2. Band Lat Pulldown (overhead anchor) *or* Band Row (if no overhead anchor)
**Target:** 3 × 12 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** Medium band × 12 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 2:** Medium band × 12 (RPE 7)
- **Set 3:** Medium band × 12 (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 60 sec  
**Notes:** Pull shoulders “down into back pockets” before bending elbows.

### 3. Kettlebell Romanian Deadlift
**Target:** 2 × 10 @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 25 lb × 10 (RPE 6)
- **Set 2:** 25 lb × 10 (RPE 6.5)  
**Rest:** 75–90 sec  
**Notes:** Soft knees, hips back, stop where hamstrings load without losing a neutral spine.

## Accessory/Conditioning Block (optional, 6–8 minutes)
### Option A (simple intervals): Marching / Step-ups
**Target:** 8 rounds (8 minutes total)  
- **Round 1–8:** 30 sec brisk march/step-ups + 30 sec easy walk/breathe  
**Intensity:** Work intervals at **RPE 6** (you can talk in short sentences)  
**Notes:** Keep it low-impact. If you feel pelvic heaviness/pressure, reduce speed and focus on exhale.

## Core (optional, 3–4 minutes)
### Side Plank (knees bent)
**Target:** 2 × 15 sec/side @ easy  
- **Set 1:** 15 sec/side
- **Set 2:** 15 sec/side  
**Rest:** 30–45 sec  
**Notes:** If any pressure/doming: shorten to **10 sec** or swap to **suitcase carry 2×20 steps/side**.

## Cool Down (3 minutes)
1. **Hip flexor stretch (half-kneeling, gentle):** 30 sec/side  
2. **Figure-4 glute stretch (on back):** 30 sec/side  
3. **Slow breathing (hands on ribs):** 5 breaths

## Notes
- If you only have 10–15 minutes: do **Goblet squat + band pull** only (superset them).
- Total time: ~27 minutes (estimated ~30).

---

# Workout — Friday, February 27, 2026
**Focus:** Day C — Single-Leg + Press Angle + Back  
**Location:** Home garage  
**Duration:** ~25–30 minutes

## Warm-Up (4 minutes)
1. **Standing hip circles (supported):** 5/side
2. **Band external rotation (elbows at sides, light band):** 12 reps
3. **Light suitcase carry:** 15 steps/side (15 lb)

## Main Workout
### 1. Split Squat (bodyweight to start)
**Target:** 3 × 8/side @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** Bodyweight × 8/side (RPE 6)
- **Set 2:** Bodyweight × 8/side (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 3:** Bodyweight × 8/side (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 60–90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Shorten range if needed. Think “elevator down/up,” torso tall. If stable and symptom-free next week, add **15 lb goblet**.

### 2. Half-Kneeling Band Press
**Target:** 3 × 10/side @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** Light band × 10/side (RPE 6)
- **Set 2:** Light band × 10/side (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 3:** Light band × 10/side (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 60 sec  
**Notes:** Squeeze glute on the down-knee side; exhale as you press.

### 3. Band Row (different grip than Wednesday—palms-down or neutral)
**Target:** 3 × 12 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** Medium band × 12 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 2:** Medium band × 12 (RPE 7)
- **Set 3:** Medium band × 12 (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 60 sec  
**Notes:** Pause 1 sec with shoulder blades back/down.

## Accessory Block (optional, ~6 minutes)
### 4. Banded Good Morning
**Target:** 2 × 15 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** Light band × 15 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 2:** Light band × 15 (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 45–60 sec  
**Notes:** Hinge pattern—feel hamstrings; keep ribs stacked.

### 5. Band Curl + Band Triceps Pressdown (superset)
**Target:** 1–2 rounds × 15 reps each @ RPE 7  
- **Round 1:** Curl 15 (RPE 7) → Pressdown 15 (RPE 7)  
- **Round 2 (optional):** Curl 15 (RPE 7) → Pressdown 15 (RPE 7)  
**Rest:** 45 sec between rounds  
**Notes:** Smooth reps, full range you can control.

## Core (optional, 3–4 minutes)
### Bird Dog (hands elevated on box if needed)
**Target:** 2 × 6/side (3-sec reach)  
- **Set 1:** 6/side
- **Set 2:** 6/side  
**Rest:** 30–45 sec  
**Notes:** Exhale as you reach; hips stay square. If wrists don’t love it, elevate hands more.

## Cool Down (3 minutes)
1. **Quad stretch (standing, light support):** 30 sec/side  
2. **Lat stretch (hands on wall, sit back):** 30 sec  
3. **Breathing reset (long exhales):** 5 breaths

## Notes
- If you only have 10 minutes: do **Split squats + band press** only.
- Total time: ~28 minutes (estimated ~30).

---

## Weekly Summary
- **Day A (Hinge/Push/Carry):** Established baseline loads and movement quality at **RPE 6–7** using **25 lb hinge/row + 15 lb floor press**; core stayed in low-pressure patterns (dead bug + suitcase carry).
- **Day B (Squat/Pull/Conditioning):** Consistent squat patterning to a box/target and an easy **8-minute low-impact interval** option to support fat loss without long sessions.
- **Day C (Single-leg/Press/Back):** Bodyweight split squat baseline to build confidence and balance first; half-kneeling press reinforces stacked posture and controlled breathing.
- **Constraint monitoring (postpartum core):** All core choices are symptom-guided; default scales were provided (shorter holds, smaller range, carries-only swap).
- **Next week (Week 2):** Keep loads the same and aim for **+1 rep per set** on the main lifts *if* RPE stays ≤7 and core symptoms stay quiet; otherwise repeat Week 1 exactly (repeat weeks still count as progress for consistency).

If you tell me what kettlebell weights you actually have (e.g., 10/15/20/25/35 lb) and whether dead bugs/carries feel 100% symptom-free, I’ll “lock” the exact starting loads and tighten the progression for Week 2.</Week>

reference.response:
Day C — Full Body (Single-Leg + Press + Back) 💪

Warm-Up:
- Standing hip circles (supported): 5/side
- Band external rotation: x12
- Light suitcase carry: 15 steps/side @ 15 lb

Workout:
- Split squat (BW): 3x8/side
- Half-kneeling band press (light): 3x10/side
- Band row (medium): 3x12
- Banded good morning (light): 2x15
- Band curl + band triceps pressdown: 1–2x15 each
- Bird dog: 2x6/side (3s reach)

Notes: Keep effort around RPE 6–7 and exhale on presses/hinges—no doming, heaviness/pressure, pain, or leaking. If you only have 10 min, do split squats + band press and call it a win.
